decks.notes: This deck is focused on quickly dealing damage to the opponent through a combination of direct damage spells and efficient creatures. By using cards like Lightning Bolt, Incinerate, and Fireblast, along with aggressive creatures like Mogg Fanatic and Jackal Pup, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and kill them before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Grim Lavamancer and Sulfuric Vortex also helps to maintain pressure and prevent the opponent from gaining life or stabilizing the board.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly set up a powerful combo using cards like Replenish and Opalescence to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of enchantments. By utilizing cards like Intuition and Attunement to search for key pieces, the deck can quickly assemble its combo and lock down the opponent with cards like Decree of Silence and Parallax Wave. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Meddling Mage and Tsabo's Web to control the opponent's plays and secure victory through its powerful synergies.

Utilizing a combination of mana denial and creature control, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while quickly ramping up its own board presence. By utilizing cards like Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore, the deck can quickly overwhelm the opponent with strong threats. Additionally, cards like Sphere of Resistance slow down the opponent's ability to cast spells, giving this control deck the time it needs to establish a dominant position on the battlefield.

The deck focuses on quick, aggressive creature-based strategies with direct burn spells to finish off opponents. By playing low-cost creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic early on, and following up with high-damage spells like Ball Lightning and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to rapidly deplete the opponent's life total. Cursed Scroll provides additional direct damage, while cards like Fireblast and Incinerate ensure that the pressure stays on the opponent. Mishra's Factory and Sulfuric Vortex provide additional support and damage to secure a swift victory.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying multiple goblin creatures to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to quickly bring out powerful creatures like Goblin Piledriver and Goblin Ringleader, the deck aims to apply pressure early in the game. With support cards like Rishadan Port and Gempalm Incinerator to disrupt the opponent's strategy, this deck aims to maintain control of the battlefield and quickly close out games with a swarm of goblin creatures.

The main theme of this deck is to control the opponent's board through countering spells and removal, while also utilizing a "standstill" strategy to generate card advantage. The deck aims to slow down the game by controlling the opponent's resources, using lands like Flooded Strand and Dust Bowl to further disrupt their game plan. It also includes a mix of creature removal and board wipes like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God to clear threats. Additionally, it includes a win condition in the form of Decree of Justice tokens or beatdown with creatures like Mishra?s Factory. It focuses on outlasting and outvaluing the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays, utilizing efficient creatures like Jackal Pup, Mogg Fanatic, and Quirion Dryad. The deck aims to control the board with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate, while also generating value through cards like Lava Dart and Call of the Herd. The mana base is streamlined to ensure consistent casting of threats and removal spells, with some utility lands like Treetop Village and Phyrexian Furnace for added versatility. Overall, the deck looks to pressure the opponent early and maintain tempo throughout the game.

This deck aims to assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to create a powerful threat on the battlefield. The deck includes cards like Counterspell and Foil to protect the combo pieces, as well as card draw and manipulation spells like Opt and Portent to find them quickly. Other disruptive spells like Daze and Chain of Vapor help control the game until the combo can be assembled. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficiently stifling the opponent's actions while setting up a game-winning combo.

The deck focuses on generating card advantage and disrupting the opponent's game plan through countermagic, hand disruption, and removal spells. It utilizes Psychatog as a versatile threat that can quickly close out games. The deck also includes cantrips and card draw spells to maintain consistency and ensure access to key pieces. By controlling the board and maintaining card advantage, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and secure victory through incremental advantage. The inclusion of a variety of disruption spells ensures that the deck can handle a wide range of threats and strategies.

The deck focuses on creating a lockdown by using Stasis along with cards like Forsaken City and Gush to keep the opponent's resources tapped down. It also includes various spells like Chain of Vapor and Thwart to control the board and disruption, as well as counterspells to protect the combo. Additionally, it includes card draw and manipulation tools like Impulse and Portent to find key pieces of the combo. The deck aims to stall the game until it can win through attrition or utilizing the Black Vise win condition.

The main strategy of this deck focuses on utilizing the powerful combo of Replenish and Opalescence to overwhelm opponents with a board full of enchantments. Cards like Decree of Silence and Parallax Wave help control the board while also setting up for the combo. The deck also includes a mix of card draw and countermagic to protect the combo and disrupt opponents' game plans. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and provide early acceleration with cards like Ancient Tomb.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's hand and game plan. With a mix of efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade, alongside disruptive spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy, the deck aims to apply pressure and strip away resources from the opponent. Utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Mishra's Factory for additional mana acceleration and versatility, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently. With a focus on aggression and disruption, this Mono Black Aggro deck is geared towards a fast and relentless playstyle.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly load up the graveyard with high-powered creatures, then bring them back into play using reanimation spells such as Animate Dead and Reanimate. These creatures, such as Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Rorix Bladewing, provide a powerful board presence that can overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes disruption elements like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own combo. Overall, the key strategy revolves around graveyard recursion and powerful creature threats to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to use the power of enchantments to create a combo that generates advantage through card draw or removal. By utilizing cards like Enchantress's Presence, Serra's Sanctum, and Wild Growth to fuel the engine, the deck aims to cast powerful enchantments like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. With a focus on enchantment synergy and card advantage, the deck can quickly establish a dominant board presence and control the game.

The main focus of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to quickly and efficiently put pressure on the opponent by playing low-cost, aggressive creatures such as Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade. With disruptive elements like Cabal Therapy and Duress, as well as removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict, the deck aims to control the opponent's resources while continuing to present a threat. The inclusion of cards like Cursed Scroll and Drain Life provide additional ways to deal damage and close out the game swiftly. This deck thrives on its ability to maintain a consistent stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using low-cost counterspells and disruption spells to protect the combo of casting Phyrexian Dreadnought and then Stifling its comes into play trigger, allowing for a quick and powerful win condition. The deck aims to control the game through counter magic and card draw until it can assemble the combo to win the game in a fast and efficient manner.
